title: load_extra_css reads files from disk on every page request
description: activitypub.rs load_extra_css() is called on every HTML page view (index_page, profile_page, post_page). It calls load_theme_css() which reads and parses a JSON file from disk, then reads a second CSS file from disk. Under load, this means filesystem I/O + JSON parse + string allocations per request. The CSS content only changes when an admin edits the config files, so it should be loaded once at startup (or cached with a file-watcher / config reload signal). What goes wrong: unnecessary filesystem I/O and allocations on every page request; under concurrent load this serializes on filesystem locks and wastes CPU on repeated JSON parsing.
